items.headline: U.S. Equities Mixed Amid Tech Volatility and Economic Data
items.summary: U.S. stock indexes displayed mixed performance on Thursday, June 26, 2026, with the Dow Jones posting a modest gain while the Nasdaq Composite marked its fourth consecutive loss. Overall market sentiment was volatile, influenced by a significant decline in Apple shares, offsetting a semiconductor rally driven by strong Micron earnings. Investors continue to weigh inflation data and the Federal Reserve's stance on interest rates, alongside ongoing concerns about AI infrastructure costs.

items.statContext: Ended little changed, marking a fourth straight loss.
Fell 0.5% for its fourth consecutive loss.
Nasdaq 100 futures down 1.1%, S&P 500 futures down 0.47%, Dow futures down 0.08%.

items.headline: Micron Technology Soars on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Micron Technology surged after reporting better-than-expected earnings and guidance, keeping the AI memory narrative strong.

items.headline: SanDisk Jumps Amid Chip Rally
items.summary: Memory chipmaker SanDisk saw a significant gain, benefiting from upbeat sentiment in the semiconductor sector.

items.headline: Applied Materials Advances
items.summary: Applied Materials also moved higher, part of the broader strength seen in chip-related stocks.

items.headline: Apple Drops on Price Hikes and Cost Concerns
items.summary: Apple shares fell sharply after the company raised prices across its Macs, iPads, and other devices, sparking worries about memory-chip cost pressures impacting demand.

items.headline: Jefferies Slides After Earnings Miss
items.summary: Jefferies Group declined after its latest earnings report missed analyst estimates.

items.headline: SoftBank Group Leads Asian Tech Selloff
items.summary: SoftBank Group shares experienced a significant drop in Tokyo trading, contributing to a broader tech selloff in Asian markets.

items.headline: May PCE Inflation Aligns with Expectations, Fed Remains Cautious
items.summary: The Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index for May rose 0.4% month-over-month, bringing the annual rate to 4.1%, which was below the 0.5% consensus estimate. Core PCE increased 0.3% month-over-month and 3.4% year-over-year. Despite some hopeful signs, Federal Reserve officials, including Goolsbee and Williams, cautioned that inflation remains too high, with markets pricing a 47% probability of a September rate hike.

items.headline: AI-Driven Tech Sector Sees Divergent Performance
items.summary: The AI-related technology sector experienced mixed movements. While memory chipmakers like Micron and SanDisk rallied strongly due to robust AI demand and earnings beats, other large tech companies, including Apple, Nvidia, Microsoft, and Alphabet, faced selling pressure amid concerns about rising AI infrastructure costs and profit-taking. Reports of OpenAI potentially delaying its IPO until 2027 further contributed to the tech sector's volatility.

items.headline: Industrials Sector Leads Gains, Consumer Discretionary Lags
items.summary: The Industrials sector showed strength, reaching a record high with notable contributions from companies like Caterpillar and Deere. Conversely, the Consumer Discretionary sector lagged, following a downward revision of first-quarter consumer spending growth to 0.5% from 1.4%, signaling consumer pressures.

items.headline: Oil Prices Under Pressure Amid Supply Ease, Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Crude oil prices declined and were set for weekly losses as easing supply concerns related to the Strait of Hormuz outweighed reports of a cargo vessel being hit near Oman. Geopolitical events in the Middle East continue to introduce volatility into energy markets.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports: Micron Shines, Jefferies Misses
items.summary: Micron Technology ($MU) delivered a strong fiscal third-quarter earnings report, exceeding analyst expectations and boosting the semiconductor industry. In contrast, Jefferies missed its earnings estimates, leading to a decline in its stock price. Beyond Air Inc. ($XAIR) and Apogee Enterprises Inc. ($APOG) also reported earnings today.
